title: pentas schimperiana subsp. occidentalis
intro: Pentas schimperiana subsp. occidentalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the wild pentas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The plant's health benefits include the treatment of fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an anti-hemorrhagic agent. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Pentas schimperiana subsp. occidentalis are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures for medicinal purposes.
